title: ds-viz-line — option matrix (light)
description: Test bench for splunk.line. Each panel exercises a distinct option group. SPL is makeresults-based so this dashboard runs in any Splunk instance with no setup. Convention codified for time-series charts: hide the x-axis title (the word '_time' is noise), keep timestamp labels visible (auto), and hide the major tick marks (visual noise). Re-enable elements only when they carry information.

options.query: | makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval requests = round(100 + 30*sin(i/3) + random()%20) | table _time requests

| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval us_east = round(80 + 20*sin(i/4) + random()%10), us_west = round(60 + 25*sin(i/5+1) + random()%10), eu_west = round(45 + 15*sin(i/3+2) + random()%8) | table _time us_east us_west eu_west

| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval allowed = round(900 + 100*sin(i/4) + random()%50), blocked = round(40 + 25*sin(i/3) + random()%15), suspicious = round(15 + 10*sin(i/5) + random()%8) | table _time allowed blocked suspicious

| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval requests_per_min = round(800 + 200*sin(i/4) + random()%80), p95_latency_ms = round(120 + 80*sin(i/3+1) + random()%30) | table _time requests_per_min p95_latency_ms

| makeresults count=30 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-30d@d") + (i*86400) | eval revenue = case(i<10, round(8000+random()%500), i>=10 AND i<20, round(15000+1500*sin(i/2)+random()%800), 1==1, round(11000+random()%600)) | table _time revenue

| makeresults count=3 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = case(i=1, relative_time(now(), "-21d@d"), i=2, relative_time(now(), "-14d@d"), 1==1, relative_time(now(), "-7d@d")), annotationLabel = case(i=1, "Black Friday", i=2, "Christmas Push", 1==1, "Boxing Day"), annotationColor = case(i=1, "#FF2D95", i=2, "#FFB627", 1==1, "#7AA2FF") | table _time annotationLabel annotationColor

| makeresults count=30 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-30d@d") + (i*86400) | eval traffic = round(pow(10, 1+(i/6)) + random()%100), errors = if(i%5=0, null(), round(pow(10, (i/8)) + random()%5)) | table _time traffic errors

| makeresults count=20 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-20h") + (i*3600) | eval target = 100, actual = round(70 + 40*sin(i/3) + random()%20), forecast = round(85 + 30*sin(i/4+1) + random()%15) | table _time target actual forecast

| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval cpu_pct = round(40 + 30*sin(i/4) + random()%10), memory_gb = round(8 + 4*sin(i/5+1) + random()%2*0.1, 1), disk_iops = round(2000 + 1500*sin(i/3+2) + random()%500) | table _time cpu_pct memory_gb disk_iops
| makeresults count=40 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-40h") + (i*3600) | eval pulse = round(50 + 30*sin(i/3) + random()%10) | table _time pulse

| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval cpu_pct = round(40 + 30*sin(i/4) + random()%10), memory_pct = round(55 + 20*sin(i/5+1) + random()%8), latency_p50 = round(40 + 20*sin(i/3) + random()%10), latency_p95 = round(120 + 60*sin(i/3+1) + random()%30) | table _time cpu_pct memory_pct latency_p50 latency_p95

| makeresults count=20 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-20h") + (i*3600) | eval solid_line = round(80 + 5*sin(i/3)), dash_line = round(70 + 5*sin(i/3+0.5)), dot_line = round(60 + 5*sin(i/3+1)), shortDash_line = round(50 + 5*sin(i/3+1.5)), longDash_line = round(40 + 5*sin(i/3+2)), dashDot_line = round(30 + 5*sin(i/3+2.5)) | table _time solid_line dash_line dot_line shortDash_line longDash_line dashDot_line

| makeresults count=48 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-48h") + (i*3600) | eval signal = round(100 + 50*sin(i/6) + random()%15), baseline = 100 | table _time signal baseline

options.markdown:
# `splunk.line` — option matrix (light)

Each panel exercises one option group. **Convention for every time-series panel:** `xAxisTitleVisibility:"hide"` (the word `_time` is noise), `xAxisLabelVisibility:"auto"` (timestamps stay visible), `xAxisMajorTickVisibility:"hide"` (no tick marks under the axis).

viz_basic.title: 1. Minimal — clean defaults applied
viz_basic.description: Bare options: x-title hidden, x-labels auto (visible), tick marks hidden. This is the recommended baseline for every time-series line chart.

viz_multiseries.title: 2. Multi-series — seriesColors[]
viz_multiseries.description: Explicit seriesColors array (positional). Order in SPL output = order in array.

viz_byfield.title: 3. Named colors — seriesColorsByField{}
viz_byfield.description: Field-name → color map. Survives SPL field reorder. Use for semantic colors (allowed=green, blocked=red).

viz_dualaxis.title: 4. Dual y-axis — overlayFields + y2
viz_dualaxis.description: p95_latency_ms mapped to right y-axis. Separates units that should not share a scale.

viz_annotations.title: 5. Event annotations — separate annotation dataSource
viz_annotations.description: Annotation marks come from a SECOND dataSource named 'annotation'. Required fields: _time, annotationLabel, annotationColor.

viz_log_nulls.title: 6. Log scale + null handling
viz_log_nulls.description: yAxisScale=log compresses orders of magnitude. nullValueDisplay=connect bridges gaps in the errors series. Minor gridlines OFF on log scale (would produce one line per minor tick).

viz_dashstyles.title: 7. Per-field dash styles + markers
viz_dashstyles.description: lineDashStylesByField: target=dot (reference line), forecast=dash (predicted), actual=solid (real). markerDisplay=filled adds dots at every value.

viz_split.title: 8. Split series — independent y-ranges
viz_split.description: showSplitSeries=true stacks each series in its own sub-chart. showIndependentYRanges=true gives each its own y-axis range.

viz_hidden_chrome.title: 9. Hidden chrome — sparkline (everything off)
viz_hidden_chrome.description: Sparkline pattern: titles hidden, BOTH axis labels hidden, tick marks hidden, gridlines off, legend off. The line is the only signal. Use inside KPI tiles or executive cards.

viz_overlays.title: 10. Multi-overlay — 4 fields with mixed axes
viz_overlays.description: Two p50/p95 latency fields overlaid on y2 (right axis), two pct fields on primary y. overlayFields can be array. y2AxisTitleText labels the right axis.

viz_all_dashes.title: 11. All 6 dash styles — one per series
viz_all_dashes.description: solid / dash / dot / shortDash / longDash / dashDot — every supported lineDashStyle value side by side. Use this panel as the visual reference when picking a dash style.

viz_smart_axis.title: 12. Smart axis — recommended baseline
viz_smart_axis.description: Clean defaults pattern. x-title hidden (no '_time'), x-labels auto (timestamps visible), x major ticks hidden. y-axis title carries unit. Legend at bottom for compactness.
